title: MeshCore Analyzer observer without a Linux gateway

messages.author: Matiko
messages.text: In my opinion, it would be a great feature for WiFi-based observers (like the XIAO S3) to work standalone without needing a Linux master node. Looking at the Flash and RAM usage, the WiFi stack and MQTT client should fit easily. This would allow for deploying more observers in areas with better coverage at a much lower cost. Would anyone like to help me bring this idea to life? The main hurdle would be storing WiFi credentials, but we should be able to handle that neatly using NVS.
messages.content_html: In my opinion, it would be a great feature for WiFi-based observers (like the XIAO S3) to work standalone without needing a Linux master node. Looking at the Flash and RAM usage, the WiFi stack and MQTT client should fit easily. This would allow for deploying more observers in areas with better coverage at a much lower cost. Would anyone like to help me bring this idea to life? The main hurdle would be storing WiFi credentials, but we should be able to handle that neatly using NVS.
